name: W boson mass

observable.description: Mass of the W boson, predicted in the Standard Model by an electroweak global fit using m_Z, the top mass, the Higgs mass, alpha_em, and G_F. Direct measurements at hadron and lepton colliders test the SM at loop level.

characteristic_scale.note: W mass itself sets the scale.

method_variants.label: Gfitter post-2018
method_variants.reference: Haller et al., Eur. Phys. J. C 78, 675 (2018), arXiv:1803.01853

references: Haller et al., Eur. Phys. J. C 78, 675 (2018), arXiv:1803.01853
Particle Data Group, Review of Particle Physics 2024

measurements.note: The headline 7-sigma anomaly. ~76 MeV above the SM and the ATLAS/CMS/LHCb measurements.
Improved analysis with PDF profiling. Consistent with SM, inconsistent with CDF II.
First CMS m_W measurement. Consistent with SM.

tension_history.comparison: CDF II vs SM EW fit
tension_history.note: Headline result. Also ~3 sigma above the pre-2022 world average.

tension_history.comparison: ATLAS+CMS+LHCb+D0+LEP avg vs SM, with CDF as outlier
tension_history.note: After ATLAS reanalysis and CMS first result, the non-CDF world average is fully consistent with the SM. CDF II remains a ~5 sigma outlier among the experimental measurements themselves.

principles_stressed.note: A CDF-like value of m_W would imply new physics contributing to the electroweak oblique parameters (primarily T), but does not violate unitarity, causality, gauge invariance, or CPT. With ATLAS+CMS+LHCb consistent with the SM, the present discrepancy is between experiments, not between the world average and the SM.

operators.name: O_HD
operators.description: (H^dagger D_mu H)^* (H^dagger D^mu H). Custodial-symmetry-breaking operator; its Wilson coefficient C_HD shifts the Peskin-Takeuchi T parameter.
operators.current_bound: EW global fits constrain C_HD / Lambda^2 at the level of ~0.05 TeV^-2 (post-2024 LHC m_W average). The CDF II value alone, if used, would prefer a ~2-3 sigma nonzero C_HD.
operators.reference: de Blas et al. (HEPfit) 2022, arXiv:2204.04204; Bagnaschi et al. 2022, arXiv:2204.05260

operators.name: O_HWB
operators.description: (H^dagger tau^I H) W^I_{mu nu} B^{mu nu}. Wilson coefficient shifts the S parameter.
operators.reference: Grzadkowski, Iskrzynski, Misiak, Rosiek, JHEP 10, 085 (2010), arXiv:1008.4884 (Warsaw basis)

oblique_parameters.S: Consistent with 0; m_W shift is dominantly a T-direction effect.
oblique_parameters.T: CDF II (alone) prefers T ~ 0.15-0.20 (3-sigma nonzero). Post-2024 world average (CDF + ATLAS + CMS + LHCb + D0 + LEP) is consistent with T = 0 within errors.
oblique_parameters.U: Consistent with 0; usually fixed to 0 in SMEFT fits.

global_fits: Bagnaschi, Ellis, Madigan, Mimasu, Sanz, You, arXiv:2204.05260

summary: CDF II's 2022 measurement of m_W gave 80,433.5 +/- 9.4 MeV, ~7 sigma above the SM electroweak fit and ~76 MeV above the previous world average. Subsequent measurements at the LHC (LHCb 2022, ATLAS reanalysis 2024, CMS first measurement 2024) all favor the SM value. The discrepancy now sits primarily *between* CDF and the LHC experiments, not between the world average and the SM. The remaining open question is the systematic source of CDF's high value.
systematics_notes: PDF uncertainties in the underlying p-pbar / pp -> W production are the dominant theoretical input to all hadronic m_W measurements. CDF's analysis used different PDF sets and recoil-modeling choices than the LHC experiments; revisiting CDF data with modern PDF profiling has been proposed but is non-trivial post-Tevatron-shutdown.

references.citation: CDF Collaboration, 'High-precision measurement of the W boson mass with the CDF II detector', Science 376, 170-176 (2022)
ATLAS Collaboration, 'Measurement of the W-boson mass and width with the ATLAS detector using proton-proton collisions at sqrt(s)=7 TeV' (2024)
CMS Collaboration, 'High-precision measurement of the W boson mass with the CMS experiment at the LHC', Nature 632, 506-511 (2024)
LHCb Collaboration, 'Measurement of the W boson mass', JHEP 01, 036 (2022)
